articles.title: Prior Year Bills
articles.summary: Transfers $2,425.51 from available FY2023 funds to pay prior-year unpaid bills across DPW, Roads, and Enterprise departments.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer $2,425.51 from available Fiscal Year 2023 funds for the purpose of paying prior year, unpaid bills: DPW Building Maintenance Repairs ($39.06), Heat/Natural Gas ($428.57), Roads and Sidewalks ($527.64), DPW Admin Public Safety Tree Work ($374.64), Parks Contracted Services ($93.60), Building Maintenance Contracted Services ($253.50), Water Enterprise Advertisement ($500.50), Solid Waste Enterprise Advertisement ($208.00), or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Free Cash Transfer to General Stabilization
articles.summary: Transfers $200,000 from Certified Free Cash to the General Stabilization Fund.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer $200,000 from Certified Free Cash to the General Stabilization Fund, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Free Cash Transfer to Tax Title Services
articles.summary: Transfers $20,000 from Certified Free Cash for tax title legal and technical services.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer $20,000 from Certified Free Cash to be expended on tax title legal and technical services, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Free Cash Transfer to Police Forfeiture Account
articles.summary: Transfers $2,824.27 from Certified Free Cash to the Police Forfeiture account.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer $2,824.27 from Certified Free Cash to the Police Forfeiture account, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Sewer Enterprise Retained Earnings Transfer
articles.summary: Transfers $165,000 from Sewer Enterprise retained earnings to fund inflow/infiltration work ($75,000) and replace a 2012 Ford F-250 truck ($90,000).
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer from Sewer Enterprise Fund retained earnings the sum of $165,000 for: Inflow and Infiltration ($75,000) and Replace 2012 Ford F-250 (Veh.) ($90,000), including associated engineering, maintenance, and legal services costs, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Water Enterprise Retained Earnings Transfer
articles.summary: Transfers $90,000 from Water Enterprise retained earnings to replace a 2012 Ford F-350 truck.
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer from Water Enterprise Fund retained earnings the sum of $90,000 for the purpose of funding the replacement of a 2012 Ford F-350 truck, including costs of fitting and equipping, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: CPA Fund Statutory Set-Aside Adjustment
articles.summary: Authorizes the Town Accountant to adjust prior-year CPA set-aside balances to meet the statutory 10% minimum — $34,215 each for community housing, open space, and historical preservation ($102,645 total).
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to authorize the Town Accountant, with CPC approval, to adjust prior fiscal year set-aside balances within the Community Preservation Fund to comply with the statutory 10% minimum requirement for open space, historic resources, and community housing. Transfers: Community Housing ($34,215), Open Space ($34,215), Historical Preservation ($34,215) = $102,645, or act in any manner relating thereto.

articles.title: Repurpose Monetary Articles: School Projects
articles.summary: Transfers $259,041.12 from unused balances of prior school capital articles to fund Memorial Cafeteria A/C ($95,000), HS Fire Alarm Panel Work ($57,450), McGovern Tile Replacement ($72,000), and HS Cafeteria Water Heater ($34,591).
articles.text: To see if the Town will vote to transfer $259,041.12 from unused balances authorized at prior Town Meetings (various school capital projects from 2021 and 2022 ATMs that were completed under budget) to fund: Memorial Cafeteria A/C Unit ($95,000 — from prior 2021 ATM Art.7 balance), High School Fire Alarm Panel Work ($57,450), McGovern Tile Replacement ($72,000), and High School Cafeteria Water Heater ($34,591.12), or act in any manner relating thereto.
